engineGuide.h2: Jeep Grand Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Jeep Grand across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: The VM Motori A428 3.0 CRD V6 became the dominant diesel in UK Grand Cherokees from WK2 onwards.
compatibleVariants: Laredo 3.0 CRD, Limited 3.0 CRD, Overland 3.0 CRD, Summit 3.0 CRD, WL 3.0 CRD
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch documented between 80k-120k miles
Oil cooler seal leaks causing coolant contamination
Turbo VGT actuator sticking on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for EHD Jeep Grand engine replacement

entries.title: 2.8L Diesel
entries.history: The 2.8 CRD appeared in facelift WK models with improved injector clamping over the earlier 2.7 unit.
compatibleVariants: WK Laredo 2.8 CRD, WK Limited 2.8 CRD
commonFailures: Water pump leaks leading to overheating
Turbo actuator wear causing boost loss
EGR cooler clogging on short-journey vehicles
entries.cta: Get quotes for ENS Jeep Grand engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: The Multijet II 2.2L four-cylinder arrived in late WK2 and WL models as a more efficient diesel option.
compatibleVariants: WK2 Laredo 2.2 Multijet, WK2 Limited 2.2 Multijet, WL 2.2 Multijet
commonFailures: Turbo VGT actuator failure documented on high-mileage units
Injector coking causing rough idle and misfires
DPF regeneration issues on predominantly urban-driven vehicles
entries.cta: Get quotes for EBU Jeep Grand engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: The Pentastar V6 replaced Chrysler's older 3.7/4.7 units, becoming the most common petrol engine in WK2 and WL Grand Cherokees.
compatibleVariants: WK2 Laredo 3.6, Limited 3.6, Overland 3.6, WL 3.6 Pentastar
commonFailures: Rocker arm bearing failure causing camshaft scoring
Oil cooler leaks leading to coolant/oil mixing
Timing chain tensioner wear causing cold-start rattles
entries.cta: Get quotes for ERB Jeep Grand engine replacement

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: The 5.7 HEMI V8 with VVT delivers strong torque for towing and remains popular in UK WK and WK2 Grand Cherokees.
compatibleVariants: WK Laredo 5.7, Limited 5.7, WK2 Limited 5.7, Overland 5.7
commonFailures: Hydraulic lifter roller collapse causing "Hemi tick" and cam damage
Exhaust manifold bolt breakage on high-mileage units
Oil consumption increase from piston ring wear after 120k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for ESD Jeep Grand engine replacement

entries.title: 6.4L Petrol
entries.history: The Apache 6.4 HEMI V8 powers the SRT performance Grand Cherokee, demanding specialist rebuild expertise.
commonFailures: Generally robust; occasional lifter wear on track-driven examples
Oil cooler leaks on high-mileage performance use
Exhaust manifold issues similar to 5.7 HEMI
entries.cta: Get quotes for EXF Jeep Grand engine replacement

Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Jeep Grand Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Jeep Grand's diesel and petrol engines are capable but not immune to known failures. The 3.0 CRD timing chain, 2.7 CRD injector seals, and 3.6 Pentastar rocker arms dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.group: Jeep Grand 3.0 CRD Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Jeep Grand 3.0 CRD Timing Chain Failure - Why VM Motori Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Grand WK2 3.0 CRD Laredo, Limited, Overland (2011-2021, EHD/EHC engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear combined with oil cooler seal leaks allows coolant contamination, accelerating chain stretch and potential jump.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chain, guides, tensioner replacement before failure; oil system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; not suitable if engine has seized

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Oil cooler gasket, seal replacement; coolant system pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause but won't fix already-stretched chain

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EHD unit with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable option if chain has jumped or engine seized; addresses all failure points

problems.recommendation: If your 3.0 CRD is rattling on cold start but still runs, a preventative chain service offers better value. If the chain has jumped or the engine has seized, replacement is the only logical choice - and a rebuilt EHD with upgraded components costs less than a dealer repair on a seized block.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EHD 3.0 CRD engine prices for your Jeep Grand

problems.h4: Jeep Grand 2.7 CRD Black Death - Injector Seal Failure on Early Diesels
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Grand WJ 2.7 CRD Laredo, Limited (1999-2004, ENR/EKG engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Copper injector seal degradation allows combustion gases to leak into the cylinder head, carbonising oil and causing injector seizure - the notorious "Black Death".

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, replace copper seals, clean carbon deposits
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught before head damage; requires specialist tools

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt ENR unit with upgraded seals, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if head is cracked or injectors seized; often cheaper than head repair on WJ

problems.recommendation: The 2.7 CRD is a robust block but the injector seal design is fundamentally flawed. If you hear a ticking or smell diesel in the oil, act immediately. On a WJ worth under £3,000, a rebuilt engine often represents better value than a head repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Grand 2.7 CRD Black Death fix

problems.group: Jeep Grand 3.6 Pentastar Rocker Arm Failure
problems.h4: Jeep Grand 3.6 Pentastar Rocker Arm Failure - The Ticking That Destroys Camshafts
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Grand WK2/WL 3.6 Pentastar Laredo, Limited, Overland (2011-present, ERB/ERC engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Rocker arm bearing wear causes metal-on-metal contact with the camshaft lobe, leading to scoring, loss of valve lift, and eventual camshaft failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace rocker arms, inspect cam lobes
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if camshaft is undamaged; requires precise torque procedures

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace camshaft, rockers, lifters; machine heads if scored
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses damage but labour-intensive; cost approaches replacement on high-mileage units

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt ERB/ERC unit with upgraded rockers, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Often the most cost-effective solution if camshaft is scored; eliminates future rocker risk

problems.recommendation: If you hear a persistent tick that increases with RPM, get a compression test immediately. If the camshaft is scored, replacement is almost always better value than a top-end rebuild on a Pentastar.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt ERB 3.6 Pentastar engine prices for your Jeep Grand

problems.h4: Jeep Grand 5.7 HEMI Lifter Failure - The "Hemi Tick" That Wrecks Camshafts
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Grand WK/WK2 5.7 HEMI Laredo, Limited, Overland (2005-2021, ESD engine code)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic lifter roller collapse causes metal-on-metal contact with the camshaft lobe, leading to scoring, loss of VVT function, and eventual engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake, replace lifters, inspect cam lobes
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if camshaft is undamaged; requires precise valve timing reset

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt ESD unit with upgraded lifters, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if camshaft is scored; eliminates future lifter risk and restores VVT function

problems.recommendation: The Hemi tick is not normal - it's a warning. If your 5.7 is ticking and losing power, get a borescope inspection. If the cam is scored, a rebuilt ESD with upgraded lifters is the only reliable long-term fix.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Grand 5.7 HEMI engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Jeep Grand
finalCta.paragraph: The problems above represent the most common failure patterns driving UK Jeep Grand replacement enquiries. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted specialists who understand these specific failure modes. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original weak point, bench testing, and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Jeep Grand Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Jeep Grand and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Jeep Grand Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Jeep Grand in the UK is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes.

items.title: Jeep Grand 2.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.7 CRD VM Motori diesel powered early WJ Grand Cherokees and remains a high-failure engine due to injector seal issues. Known as the "Black Death" engine, replacement demand is driven by catastrophic seal failure rather than routine wear.
commonFailurePoints: Injector copper seal failure causing carbon buildup
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Grand 2.7L diesel engines

items.title: Jeep Grand 2.8L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.8 CRD appeared in facelift WK models with improved injector clamping but retained water pump and turbo vulnerabilities. Less common than the 3.0 CRD but still generates replacement enquiries from high-mileage WK owners.
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Grand 2.8L diesel engines

items.title: Jeep Grand 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0 CRD VM Motori A428 is the most sought-after diesel in the UK Grand Cherokee range, powering WK2 and WL models. High replacement demand stems from timing chain and oil cooler seal failures rather than block weakness.
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Grand 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Jeep Grand 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2 Multijet II diesel arrived in late WK2 and WL models as a more efficient four-cylinder option. Replacement enquiries focus on turbo and injector failures rather than bottom-end issues.
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Grand 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Jeep Grand 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The legendary AMC 242 straight-six powered the original WJ Grand Cherokee and remains one of Jeep's most reliable engines. Replacement demand is low but steady from classic WJ owners seeking bulletproof replacements.
commonFailurePoints: Generally robust; occasional head gasket issues on neglected units
items.cta: Compare Jeep Grand 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Grand 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The Pentastar V6 is the most common petrol engine in WK2 and WL Grand Cherokees. Replacement demand is driven by the well-documented rocker arm failure that can destroy camshafts if ignored.
items.cta: Compare Jeep Grand 3.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Grand 5.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.7 HEMI V8 offers strong torque for towing and off-road use in WK and WK2 Grand Cherokees. Replacement enquiries focus on the "Hemi tick" lifter failure that can score camshafts.
items.cta: Compare Jeep Grand 5.7L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Grand 6.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 6.4 Apache HEMI V8 powers the SRT performance Grand Cherokee. Replacement demand is niche but high-value, with owners seeking specialist rebuilds that preserve performance characteristics.
commonFailurePoints: Generally robust; occasional lifter wear on high-mileage track-driven units
items.cta: Compare Jeep Grand 6.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Grand 6.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The supercharged 6.2 Hellcat V8 is exclusive to the Trackhawk performance flagship. Replacement is extremely rare but commands premium pricing due to specialist machining and supercharger integration requirements.
commonFailurePoints: Very low failure rate; replacement usually due to accident damage rather than mechanical fault

fuelTypes.h2: Jeep Grand Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Jeep Grand engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 2.7 CRD to the latest 3.6 Pentastar.

items.title: Jeep Grand Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel Grand Cherokees dominate UK replacement enquiries, with the 3.0 CRD VM Motori and 2.2 Multijet generating the highest volume of failure-led searches.
items.descriptor: Diesel Grand Cherokees dominate UK replacement enquiries, with the 3.0 CRD VM Motori and 2.2 Multijet generating the highest volume of failure-led searches.
items.familiesLabel: Common Jeep Grand Diesel Engine Families
families: EHD - 3.0 CRD V6 (WK2/WL), timing chain and oil cooler seal vulnerabilities
ENS/EC1 - 2.8 CRD (WK), water pump and turbo actuator wear patterns
EBU/LAU61 - 2.2 Multijet (late WK2/WL), turbo VGT and injector coking issues
knownFor: Strong towing capability and motorway fuel economy
Timing chain stretch on 3.0 CRD between 80k-120k miles
2.7 CRD injector seal "Black Death" failure on early WJ models
High UK replacement demand on diesel variants vs petrol
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Grand Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Jeep Grand Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Grand Cherokees feature the reliable 4.0 straight-six, the widespread Pentastar V6, and the high-performance HEMI V8 family.
items.descriptor: Petrol Grand Cherokees feature the reliable 4.0 straight-six, the widespread Pentastar V6, and the high-performance HEMI V8 family.
items.familiesLabel: Common Jeep Grand Petrol Engine Families
families: ERB/ERC - 3.6 Pentastar V6 (WK2/WL), rocker arm failure risk
ESD - 5.7 HEMI V8 (WK/WK2), lifter roller collapse known issue
EXF/ESG - 6.4 Apache HEMI (SRT), specialist performance rebuilds
4.0 I6 - AMC straight-six (WJ), generally robust and long-lasting
foundIn: WK2 (2011-2021) - 3.6 Pentastar dominant, 5.7/6.4 HEMI performance options
WL (2021-present) - 3.6 Pentastar continued, hybrid options added
knownFor: Pentastar V6 offers strong power but requires rocker arm monitoring
HEMI V8 delivers torque for towing but watch for lifter tick
4.0 straight-six is famously durable with minimal failure patterns
Petrol variants generate fewer replacement enquiries than diesel in UK
items.cta: Compare Jeep Grand Petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Grand Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: The WL-generation Grand Cherokee introduced 4xe plug-in hybrid variants, combining a 2.0L turbo petrol with electric motors for reduced emissions.
items.descriptor: The WL-generation Grand Cherokee introduced 4xe plug-in hybrid variants, combining a 2.0L turbo petrol with electric motors for reduced emissions.
families: 2.0L turbo petrol engine paired with front/rear electric motors
Electric-only range of up to 25 miles on full charge
Engine replacement requires hybrid system inspection and battery health check
importantNotes: No full hybrid (HEV) version of Grand Cherokee sold in UK
PHEV engine replacement requires specialist hybrid-certified workshop
Availability more limited than standard combustion engines

engineYears.h2: Jeep Grand Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Jeep Grand's UK production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The WJ generation introduced the controversial 2.7 CRD diesel alongside the legendary 4.0 straight-six petrol.
keyChanges: First Grand Cherokee with diesel engine option in UK market
2.7 CRD VM Motori engine introduced with injector seal vulnerability
4.0 AMC straight-six carried over as reliable petrol option
knownFor: 2.7 CRD "Black Death" injector seal failure pattern
4.0 straight-six renowned for durability and simplicity
High replacement demand on diesel units due to seal issues
WJ values rising among classic SUV enthusiasts
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999-2004 Jeep Grand replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Grand 2.7 CRD engine replacement • WJ injector seal fix • 4.0 straight six rebuilt engine

years.description: The WK facelift replaced the 2.7 CRD with improved 2.8 CRD and introduced the 3.0 CRD and 5.7 HEMI.
keyChanges: 2.8 CRD replaced 2.7 CRD with improved injector clamping
3.0 CRD VM Motori V6 introduced for first time
5.7 HEMI V8 with VVT added to petrol range
knownFor: 2.8 CRD water pump leaks remain a common failure point
3.0 CRD timing chain stretch documented on high-mileage units
5.7 HEMI lifter tick emerges as known issue after 90k miles
WK generation represents sweet spot for value vs reliability
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2010 Jeep Grand replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Grand 3.0 CRD timing chain • WK 2.8 water pump • 5.7 HEMI lifter replacement

years.description: The all-new WK2 platform brought the Pentastar 3.6 V6 and refined 3.0 CRD as standard powertrains.
keyChanges: Pentastar 3.6 V6 replaced older 3.7/4.7 petrol units
3.0 CRD EHD refined with improved oil cooling
5.7 HEMI continued with VVT and fuel-saving MDS
knownFor: Pentastar rocker arm failure emerges as dominant failure pattern
3.0 CRD oil cooler seal leaks documented on high-mileage units
WK2 represents most common Grand Cherokee on UK roads today
Strong aftermarket support for common failure repairs
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2014 Jeep Grand replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Grand Pentastar rocker arm • WK2 3.0 CRD oil cooler • 5.7 HEMI tick fix

years.description: Mid-cycle WK2 updates refined emissions systems but retained core engine architectures.
keyChanges: Euro 6 emissions compliance updates to diesel engines
Minor ECU recalibrations for fuel economy
No major mechanical changes to engine families
knownFor: Failure patterns unchanged from pre-facelift WK2
Rocker arm and timing chain issues remain dominant
Facelift models command slightly higher used values
Replacement engine availability excellent for all codes
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2017 Jeep Grand replacement engines

years.description: The efficient 2.2 Multijet four-cylinder diesel joined the range alongside continued 3.0 CRD and Pentastar options.
keyChanges: 2.2 Multijet II diesel introduced for improved fuel economy
3.0 CRD continued with minor emissions updates
knownFor: 2.2 Multijet turbo VGT actuator wear documented on high-mileage units
3.0 CRD and Pentastar failure patterns unchanged
Multijet units generate fewer replacement enquiries than 3.0 CRD
Excellent parts availability for all engine codes
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2020 Jeep Grand replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Grand 2.2 Multijet turbo • WK2 3.0 CRD replacement • Pentastar rocker arm fix

years.description: The new WL platform continued familiar engines while adding 4xe plug-in hybrid variants.
keyChanges: WL generation launched with carryover 3.6 Pentastar and 3.0 CRD
2.2 Multijet continued with updated emissions systems
4xe plug-in hybrid variants introduced with 2.0L turbo + electric motors
knownFor: Pentastar and 3.0 CRD failure patterns unchanged from WK2
4xe hybrid system requires specialist diagnosis for engine-related issues
WL models still under warranty for many UK examples
Replacement engine availability excellent for combustion units
years.cta: Get quotes for 2021-2024 Jeep Grand replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Grand WL 3.0 CRD • Pentastar rocker arm WL • 4xe hybrid engine query

faq.h2: Jeep Grand Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is it worth replacing an engine on a Jeep Grand Cherokee?
items.answer: Yes, for most WK2 and WL models. A rebuilt engine costs significantly less than buying a replacement vehicle, especially when the rest of the Grand is in good condition.
keyPoints: Rebuilt 3.0 CRD costs £1,500-£4,500 vs £15k+ for similar used Grand
WK2 and WL models have strong residual values post-repair
Supply & fit packages simplify the replacement process
12-month warranty provides peace of mind on rebuilt units
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Jeep Grand engine prices for your variant

items.question: How much to replace a Jeep Grand Cherokee engine UK?
items.answer: Costs range from £1,200 for a used 2.7 CRD to £8,500+ for a rebuilt 6.4 HEMI SRT, with supply & fit adding £600-£1,400.
keyPoints: Diesel 3.0 CRD rebuilt: £1,500-£4,500 supply only
Petrol 3.6 Pentastar rebuilt: £1,800-£4,200 supply only
Performance HEMI units command premium pricing
Labour for supply & fit typically £600-£1,400 extra
items.cta: Get a tailored Jeep Grand engine replacement quote today

items.question: What is the most common Jeep Grand Cherokee engine problem?
items.answer: Diesel owners report 3.0 CRD timing chain stretch and oil cooler leaks; petrol owners cite Pentastar rocker arm failure and HEMI lifter tick.
keyPoints: 3.0 CRD timing chain issues documented 80k-120k miles
Pentastar rocker arms can score camshafts if ignored
2.7 CRD injector seal "Black Death" affects early WJ models
All common failures addressed in rebuilt engine specifications
items.cta: Find a Jeep Grand engine specialist who understands your failure type

items.question: How long does a Jeep Grand Cherokee engine last?
items.answer: Well-maintained 3.0 CRD and Pentastar units can reach 200,000 miles, but known failure points often intervene earlier without preventative attention.
keyPoints: 3.0 CRD timing chain may need attention before 120k miles
Pentastar rocker arms should be inspected after 80k miles
Regular oil changes critical for HEMI lifter longevity
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components extend service life
items.cta: Check typical lifespan data for your Jeep Grand engine code

items.question: Can I put a different engine in my Jeep Grand Cherokee?
items.answer: Technically possible but rarely cost-effective; engine swaps require extensive fabrication, ECU reprogramming and may fail MOT emissions tests.
keyPoints: HEMI into diesel WK2 requires major wiring and ECU work
Non-factory engines may not pass UK MOT emissions standards
Insurance implications can be significant for modified powertrains
Sticking with factory-spec rebuilt units preserves value and compliance
items.cta: Compare factory-spec Jeep Grand replacement engine options

items.question: Where can I find a Jeep Grand Cherokee engine with warranty?
items.answer: Engines supplied through EngineMarket's UK specialist network include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard.
keyPoints: All rebuilt units bench-tested before dispatch
Warranty covers parts and labour at approved fitting centres
Supply & fit packages include warranty administration
Extended warranty options available on premium rebuilds
items.cta: Find a guaranteed Jeep Grand engine with UK warranty coverage

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt and used Grand Cherokee engine?
items.answer: A used engine is removed from a donor vehicle as-is; a rebuilt engine is stripped, machined, and fitted with new wear components addressing known failure points.
keyPoints: Used engines cheaper but carry unknown mileage and history
Rebuilt units include new gaskets, seals, bearings and upgraded parts
Rebuilt engines address documented failure patterns like timing chains
Warranty terms typically longer and more comprehensive on rebuilt units
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt Jeep Grand engine prices and warranty terms

items.question: Will a Jeep Grand Cherokee WK2 engine fit a WK?
items.answer: No. WK (2005-2010) and WK2 (2011-2021) use completely different engine architectures, mounting points and electronics.
keyPoints: WK uses 2.8 CRD/3.0 CRD with different ECU and wiring
WK2 Pentastar and refined 3.0 CRD not physically compatible
Mounting brackets, ancillaries and exhaust differ significantly
Always match engine code to your specific Grand generation
items.cta: Verify your Jeep Grand generation before ordering a replacement engine

items.question: Do you supply and fit Jeep Grand Cherokee engines?
items.answer: Yes. Select 'supply & fit' at checkout and our national network of approved specialists will install your engine with labour quotes provided upfront.
keyPoints: Supply & fit bundles engine price with professional installation
Labour costs typically £600-£1,400 depending on complexity
All fitting centres vetted and rated by real Grand owners
Road test and post-installation checks included as standard
items.cta: Find Jeep Grand engine supply & fit deals in your area

items.question: What is the turnaround time for a Grand Cherokee replacement engine?
items.answer: Delivery is typically 1-3 working days nationwide for in-stock rebuilt units; fitting usually takes 2-3 days depending on workshop availability.
keyPoints: Most common codes (EHD, ERB, ESD) held in UK stock
Performance units (EXF, Hellcat) may require short lead time
Supply & fit bookings scheduled around your availability
Emergency next-day delivery options available for urgent cases
items.cta: Get an instant Jeep Grand engine replacement quote with delivery estimate
